bypasses.reason: Batch update of costCalculatedAt timestamp on all ingredients for a recipe version after cost recalculation. This is a metadata-tracking field recording when costs were last computed, not a governed domain property. The actual cost mutation (RecipeVersion.updateCosts) IS governed via Manifest runtime. Using N individual RecipeIngredient.update Manifest commands for a batch timestamp stamp would be O(n) roundtrips for a pure bookkeeping operation with no business rules to enforce.
bypasses.whyRuntimeNotRequired: costCalculatedAt is an infrastructure timestamp, not a domain property. No state transitions, business rules, or governance policies apply to it. The only governed mutation in this route is RecipeVersion.updateCosts which already goes through Manifest runtime.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: Filter scoped to tenantId + recipeVersionId + deletedAt: null

bypasses.reason: Auto-provisions the tenant Account record on first request for a new Clerk org. This runs inside getTenantIdForOrg which is called by every authenticated route — including the Manifest runtime factory itself. The tenant Account does not exist yet when this write fires; Manifest runtime requires a tenantId to initialize, and tenantId IS the Account.id being created here. This is the identity bootstrap chicken-and-egg: Account must exist before any governed command can run.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: No Manifest runtime context exists yet — there is no tenant to scope to. The Account being created IS the tenant. No business rules or state transitions apply to tenant provisioning; it is a pure infrastructure bootstrap. All subsequent domain writes within this tenant do go through Manifest runtime.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: Single-row create scoped to orgId slug; no cross-tenant access possible

bypasses.reason: Auto-provisions or restores the internal User (employee) record from Clerk identity during requireCurrentUser(). This runs on every authenticated request to resolve the actor. The User record must exist before any governed command can identify who is performing the action (actor context). Migrating would create a circular dependency: requireCurrentUser -> governed command -> requireCurrentUser.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Identity bootstrap: the User record being created/restored IS the actor that Manifest runtime requires for authorization context. No governed command can execute without this User existing first. The write only sets identity fields (email, name, authUserId) — no domain state transitions. All subsequent domain mutations on this User (role changes, profile edits) go through governed commands.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All queries scoped by (tenantId, authUserId) composite unique index

bypasses.reason: App-side mirror of the API tenant.ts Account.create auto-provisioning. Same chicken-and-egg: the tenant Account must exist before any Manifest runtime context can be initialized for the app server actions.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Identical rationale to apps/api/app/lib/tenant.ts Account bypass. No tenant context exists; this IS the tenant creation.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: Single-row create scoped to orgId slug

bypasses.reason: App-side mirror of the API tenant.ts User auto-provisioning in resolveClerkUser(). Same circular dependency: User must exist before governed commands can resolve the actor. This code path runs when requireCurrentUser() is called from app server actions that subsequently invoke Manifest runtime.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Identity bootstrap, same as API tenant.ts User bypass. Only identity fields are set; no domain state transitions.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All queries scoped by (tenantId, authUserId) composite unique index

bypasses.reason: syncCurrentUser() is an auth bootstrap function that runs BEFORE a User record exists — it creates the User that subsequent governed commands require as actor context. Migrating it would create a circular dependency (requireCurrentUser -> syncCurrentUser -> governed command -> requireCurrentUser). It also doesn't supply all User.create required params (e.g. hireDate) expected by the governed command. Kept as direct Prisma per constitution §10 exception for bootstrapping identity. Note: addStaffMember and other staff actions in this file DO use governed runManifestCommand — only syncCurrentUser is bypassed.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: The function creates the actor record that Manifest runtime needs for authorization. Without this User existing, no governed command can identify who is performing the action. The write only sets identity fields (email, firstName, lastName, authUserId) and restores soft-deleted records. All subsequent User mutations in this file go through runManifestCommand.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All queries scoped by (tenantId, authUserId) or (tenantId, email) unique lookups

bypasses.reason: External supplier sync service that runs outside the Next.js request lifecycle. The SupplierSyncService is a standalone class that receives a Prisma-like interface via constructor injection — it has no access to the Manifest runtime factory, no Clerk auth context, no tenant resolution middleware, and no Next.js server action environment. It runs in background sync jobs or CLI scripts that pull catalog data from external supplier APIs. Five governed writes: 2x create (syncCatalog batch + syncChanges incremental), 2x update (syncCatalog batch + syncChanges incremental), 1x updateMany for deactivation of products no longer in supplier catalog.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: No Manifest runtime is available in this execution context. The service operates through an injected SupplierSyncDb interface, not the app's database client. Catalog sync is a data ingestion pipeline, not a user-initiated domain mutation. The updateMany (deactivate) maps to VendorCatalog.deactivate per-item but requires auth context (reason param) and would be O(N) individual commands. Business rules (pricing, availability) are enforced by downstream governed commands when the catalog data is consumed by purchase orders.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All queries scoped by tenantId + supplierId from the SupplierConnectorConfig parameter

bypasses.reason: syncStatus and offlineId are offline-sync coordination fields used by the client-side offline-first data layer. They track whether a record is pending sync, conflicted, or synced, and map client-generated IDs to server IDs. These fields are not covered by any Manifest command and have no domain semantics — they are infrastructure metadata for the offline sync protocol.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: syncStatus and offlineId are transport-layer coordination fields with no business rules or state transitions. They do not affect domain logic, calculations, or reporting. The governed fields on CycleCountRecord (quantity, notes, status) are mutated through Manifest commands. Patching syncStatus/offlineId alongside governed fields in the same update is safe because Prisma atomic updates only touch the specified fields.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All updates scoped by tenantId from requireCurrentUser() context

bypasses.reason: Simulation forking is a deep-copy operation that clones an existing board plus 3 child entity types (projections, groups, annotations) in a single atomic transaction. BoardProjection and BoardAnnotation have no Manifest entity definitions. createMany bulk inserts have no governed equivalent. The entire fork must be atomic — per-item governed commands would break transactional guarantees and be O(n) roundtrips for each child table.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Structural copy operation, not a domain mutation. Creates a derivative board from an existing one — no business rules, state transitions, or policy decisions are involved. The source board's governed state is preserved; the fork is a mechanical copy. CommandBoardGroup and CommandBoard annotations are presentation-layer entities with no Manifest IR coverage.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All queries scoped by tenantId; source board ownership verified via requireCurrentUser

bypasses.reason: Batch inventory operations (bulk status changes, category assignments) use updateMany for efficiency. No bulk Manifest command exists. Migrating to N individual InventoryItem.update commands would change O(1) to O(N) roundtrips and lose atomicity of the batch update.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Bulk metadata updates (status, category) with no per-item business rule evaluation. The individual item mutations ARE governed when done through single-item routes. The batch operation applies the same change to all matching items — no per-item policy decisions.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All updates scoped by tenantId from requireTenantId() context

bypasses.reason: Event document import is a multi-entity bulk create pipeline that parses uploaded files (Excel/CSV) and creates Recipes, Dishes, Events, and EventImport records in a coordinated transaction. Each entity depends on the IDs generated by the previous step. Migrating to governed commands would require sequential runManifestCommand calls with ID pass-through, losing atomicity and adding O(N) overhead per imported entity.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Data ingestion pipeline, not user-initiated domain mutations. The import process creates draft records that are subsequently reviewed and confirmed through governed commands. The initial creation has no business rules to enforce — it's translating file data into database records.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All creates scoped by tenantId from requireCurrentUser() context

bypasses.entity: Recipe, Dish, BattleBoard, Event, EventReport, EventImport
bypasses.reason: API-side document parsing creates multiple entities (Recipe, Dish, BattleBoard, Event, EventReport, EventImport) from parsed document data. Same multi-entity bulk create pattern as events/importer.ts. BattleBoard inherits parent Event context but the parsing route pre-creates records before event context is established.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Same rationale as events/importer.ts — data ingestion pipeline creating draft records. Multi-entity atomicity required.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All creates scoped by tenantId from auth context

bypasses.reason: Kitchen data import creates Recipe + RecipeVersion + Dish + PrepList + PrepListItem in a coordinated batch. Each entity depends on IDs from previous creates. Multi-entity bulk import with no governed batch equivalent.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Data ingestion pipeline for kitchen setup/onboarding. Creates initial records that are subsequently managed through governed commands.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All creates scoped by tenantId from auth context

bypasses.reason: Calendar sync creates Events from external calendar providers and updates ProviderSync state. ProviderSync is an infrastructure entity tracking sync state (last sync time, sync token) with no Manifest definition. Event creation from calendar sync imports event data from external sources before Manifest context can validate it.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: System-to-system calendar integration. ProviderSync is infrastructure. Event creation from external calendar data requires pre-processing that doesn't fit the governed command lifecycle.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All operations scoped by tenantId from auth context

bypasses.reason: Dual-write pattern: the route first resets all lead scores to 0 (line 142), then recalculates and persists per-lead scores (line 196). The governed Manifest command handles entity-level mutations, but score and scoreBreakdown are computed fields not present in the Lead IR entity definition. The scoring engine produces these derived values and persists them alongside governed state. Using individual governed Lead.update commands would require adding score/scoreBreakdown to the IR entity and issuing N sequential commands for what is a batch computation with no per-item business rules.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: score and scoreBreakdown are derived/computed fields from the scoring engine, not domain properties with business rules or state transitions. The governed Lead entity mutations (status changes, assignment, etc.) still go through Manifest runtime. The batch score update is O(N) leads in a single updateMany — migrating to per-lead governed commands would be N roundtrips for pure computed values.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All updates scoped by tenantId from requireTenantId() context

bypasses.reason: RecipeIngredient IR entity does not cover cost computation fields (adjustedQuantity, ingredientCost, costCalculatedAt). No Manifest command exists for updating these computed cost values. The updateMany at line 157 persists the calculated cost for a single ingredient after unit conversion and waste factor application. RecipeVersion and Event writes in the same module already go through governed commands.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: adjustedQuantity, ingredientCost, and costCalculatedAt are computed/derived fields from the cost calculation engine — not domain properties with business rules or state transitions. The governed RecipeIngredient mutations (if any) would be for domain changes like quantity or ingredient swaps, not for cost computation results.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All updates scoped by tenantId + id

bypasses.reason: App-side mirror of apps/api/app/lib/recipe-costing.ts. Same RecipeIngredient cost computation bypass — adjustedQuantity, ingredientCost, costCalculatedAt are not in the RecipeIngredient IR entity. The app-side recipe-costing module performs the same unit conversion and cost calculation, persisting results via direct Prisma.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Identical rationale to apps/api/app/lib/recipe-costing.ts bypass. Computed cost fields, not governed domain properties.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All updates scoped by tenantId + id

bypasses.reason: EventDish serviceStyle field has no dedicated IR command. Available commands are create, remove, updateQuantity, updateCourse, updateNotes — none can set serviceStyle. The updateEventDishLink function (line 421) patches serviceStyle alongside quantity/course/instructions during document parsing. Migrating to governed commands would silently drop the serviceStyle field. The EventDish.create path (line 460) already goes through Manifest runtime but passes serviceStyle as an extra field to the store.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: serviceStyle is not covered by any EventDish IR command — there is no governed pathway for this field. The other fields (quantityServings, course, specialInstructions) are co-patched for atomicity in the document parse pipeline. When EventDish gains an update or updateServiceStyle command, this bypass should be revisited.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All updates scoped by tenantId + id + eventId

bypasses.reason: createDishVariantForEvent creates a new Dish variant via governed Dish.create command, then swaps the EventDish link's dishId to point to the new variant. No EventDish.updateDishId command exists — the available commands (create, remove, updateQuantity, updateCourse, updateNotes) cannot change dishId. The Dish.create itself IS governed; only the subsequent dishId reassignment is direct Prisma.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: No governed command can update dishId on an existing EventDish link. The alternative (remove + create) would lose the link's other properties (quantity, course, notes, sortOrder). The swap is a structural reassignment with no business rules to enforce — the new dishId is the freshly governed-created variant.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All updates scoped by tenantId + id + eventId

bypasses.reason: Six direct Prisma writes for CommandBoardCard bulk operations: (1) bulkUpdateCardsAction updateMany for status/color/cardType changes across N cards, (2) bulkRestoreCardsAction $transaction with N individual update calls for undo/restore, (3) createGroupAction updateMany to assign groupId to cards after governed group creation, (4) ungroupCardsAction updateMany to null groupId, (5) assignToGroupAction updateMany to set groupId on existing cards, (6) deleteGroupAction updateMany to unassign cards before governed group removal. No bulk Manifest command exists for CommandBoardCard — CommandBoardCard.update operates on a single instance. Migrating would require N sequential governed commands, losing atomicity guarantees and changing O(1) batch operations to O(N) roundtrips. The bulkRestoreCardsAction uses $transaction for multi-card atomicity which has no Manifest equivalent. Explicitly deferred per IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md (lines 314, 517-604 — complex multi-model transactions with optimistic concurrency). The single-card governed writes (createCommandBoard, moveCardAction, group CRUD) all correctly use runManifestCommand and are NOT bypassed.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: All six writes are bulk operations with no per-item business rule evaluation. No bulk governed command exists for CommandBoardCard. The simulation apply/merge operations involve optimistic concurrency patterns that require direct Prisma transaction control. Single-card mutations (move, create) already go through Manifest runtime. The bypassed writes only touch structural/presentation fields (status, color, cardType, groupId) — no financial, regulatory, or irreversible domain state.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All queries scoped by tenantId from requireCurrentUser()/requireTenantId() context; updateMany further scoped by deletedAt: null

bypasses.reason: Multi-entity bulk creation pipeline for event setup. Creates EventDish, PrepList, EventContract, and EventBudget records via raw SQL INSERT in a coordinated flow. Event staff assignment (line 277) correctly uses runManifestCommand, but the other entity creation steps use $executeRaw because they create multiple records in sequence with dependent IDs. Each entity type has governed create commands, but the setup-event flow creates N records per type in a single coordinated operation — migrating to N individual governed commands would change O(1) batch operations to O(N) roundtrips and lose the coordination guarantees of the sequential flow.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Bulk creation pipeline for event initialization, not user-initiated individual domain mutations. The staff assignment step already uses governed runtime. The raw SQL creates records with default/seed values that are subsequently managed through governed commands. All writes are scoped to the event's tenantId.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All writes scoped by tenantId from requireCurrentUser() context; event ownership verified via eventId

bypasses.method: database.$executeRaw (UPDATE generationDurationMs backfill, soft-delete via UPDATE deleted_at)
bypasses.reason: Two raw SQL writes: (1) generationDurationMs backfill at line 368 — this field has no IR property/param and is not part of any EventSummary command. (2) Soft-delete at line 393 — EventSummary IR entity has no softDelete command. The inline comment confirms: 'No softDelete command in EventSummary IR'. EventSummary.create IS governed (migrated in Task 8.3 batch 7).
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: generationDurationMs is an infrastructure timing metric with no domain semantics. The soft-delete is a standard pattern that needs a softDelete command added to the EventSummary manifest source (deferred migration). The create path already uses governed runtime.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All writes scoped by tenantId + eventId

bypasses.method: database.$executeRaw (13 raw SQL writes for recipe/dish CRUD operations)
bypasses.reason: Extensive raw SQL for complex recipe management: recipe duplication with versioning, bulk ingredient operations, cross-entity coordinated updates that span Recipe + RecipeVersion + RecipeIngredient + Dish. Many operations create N records with interdependent IDs in a single flow. The governed commands exist for individual entity creates/updates but don't support batch operations, version duplication, or cross-entity atomicity patterns needed here.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Complex multi-entity coordination patterns that require transactional atomicity across 4+ entity types. Individual governed commands exist but don't compose into batch/versioning operations. The recipe module uses raw SQL for structural operations (duplicate version, bulk ingredient swap) where per-item governed commands would lose atomicity and be O(N) roundtrips.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All writes scoped by tenantId from requireCurrentUser() context

bypasses.method: database.$executeRaw (2 raw SQL writes for prep-list operations)
bypasses.reason: Raw SQL for prep-list operations that involve cross-entity coordination between PrepList and PrepListItem. The operations create/update N PrepListItem records associated with a PrepList, requiring coordinated IDs and atomicity. Individual governed commands exist for PrepList.create but batch PrepListItem creation has no governed equivalent.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Batch creation/update of PrepListItem records requires atomic coordination with the parent PrepList. No bulk governed command exists for PrepListItem. The structural operations (bulk assign, reorder) have no Manifest command equivalent.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All writes scoped by tenantId from requireCurrentUser() context

bypasses.method: database.$executeRaw (4 raw SQL writes for cleanup operations)
bypasses.reason: Cleanup operations that remove/orphan recipe data across multiple entity types. These are destructive operations that cascade across Recipe + Dish + RecipeIngredient in a coordinated fashion. Cleanup has no governed command equivalent — it's an administrative maintenance operation, not a domain mutation.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Administrative cleanup operations with no domain business rules. The cleanup removes orphaned/unused data that was created through governed paths. No state transitions, approvals, or policy decisions are involved.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: All writes scoped by tenantId from requireCurrentUser() context

bypasses.reason: CycleCountAuditLog has no Manifest entity definition or commands. It is an append-only audit trail table that records cycle count session completion events. The inline TODO at line 310 acknowledges: 'CycleCountAuditLog has no Manifest entity/commands.' The governed CycleCountSession.finalize command IS used for the main entity mutation — this bypass only covers the supplementary audit log creation.
bypasses.whyBypassSafe: Append-only audit log with no business rules, state transitions, or governance policies. The audit log records the fact that a finalization occurred — it is infrastructure metadata, not domain state. The governed CycleCountSession.finalize command handles the actual domain mutation.
bypasses.tenantBoundary: Create scoped by tenantId from requireCurrentUser() context
